water_temp_read.read: Water is warming; trout may favor more oxygenated water.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Chattahoochee River offers fair to good trout fishing with warming water temperatures favoring oxygenated spots. Recent stocking enhances success using stocked-trout attractors and natural patterns.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning provides the best window due to calmer winds, low sky cover, and stable humidity, creating ideal conditions for trout to feed actively in riffles and oxygenated runs.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 62.8°F with a flow of 899 cfs and a gage height of 2.03 ft. Turbidity is low at 5.9 NTU, indicating relatively clear water.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current weather has 20% sky cover with pressure at 30.05 inHg and a neutral pressure trend. Winds average 5.8 mph from the west with a 27% chance of light rain totaling 0.02 inches expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will likely hold in riffles, pocket water, and runs with good oxygenation. Present clean drifts using eggs, squirmy worms, and duracell attractors to match stocked trout preferences. Focus on early and late windows with active nymph tactics for best results.
